understanding.goal_understanding: The objective is to navigate through a grid-based puzzle, using modifier tiles to adjust the carried piece's shape, color, and rotation until they match the requirements of goal cells, then step on those cells to complete the level. Completing all goals in a level advances to the next, and winning all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The player occupies a tile on a grid. Each turn, they can move in one of four cardinal directions. Moving typically costs 1 stamina; if blocked by a wall, movement fails but may still cost stamina (the rules are ambiguous from the trace). The carried piece has three attributes: shape (changed by cycling through options via shape modifier tiles), color (cycled via color modifier tiles), and rotation (rotated 90° clockwise via rotation modifier tiles). Goal cells specify required shape, color, and rotation. To complete a goal, the piece's attributes must match all requirements exactly, and the player must move onto the goal cell. Stamina has a maximum (apparently 42) and is lost on each action. When stamina reaches 0, the player loses a life and stamina is reset to full. Losing all lives ends the game. Levels may contain multiple goal cells.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The objective is to navigate through a grid-based puzzle, using modifier tiles to adjust the carried piece's shape, color, and rotation until they match the requirements of goal cells, then step on those cells to complete the level. Completing all goals in a level advances to the next, and winning all levels completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The player occupies a tile on a grid. Each turn, they can move in one of four cardinal directions. Moving typically costs 1 stamina; if blocked by a wall, movement fails but may still cost stamina (the rules are ambiguous from the trace). The carried piece has three attributes: shape (changed by cycling through options via shape modifier tiles), color (cycled via color modifier tiles), and rotation (rotated 90° clockwise via rotation modifier tiles). Goal cells specify required shape, color, and rotation. To complete a goal, the piece's attributes must match all requirements exactly, and the player must move onto the goal cell. Stamina has a maximum (apparently 42) and is lost on each action. When stamina reaches 0, the player loses a life and stamina is reset to full. Losing all lives ends the game. Levels may contain multiple goal cells.
